question: How many days of PTO do full-time salaried employees get at Orion?
reference_answer: Full-time exempt (salaried) employees have a flexible PTO policy with no preset cap on days. They are expected to take at least 15 days off per year and are encouraged to take 20-25 days. PTO must be scheduled with manager approval.
expected_chunk: Orion provides a flexible PTO policy for all full-time exempt employees, meaning there is no preset cap on PTO days. Employees are expected to take at least 15 days off per year and are encouraged to take 20-25 days. PTO must be scheduled with manager approval, taking into account business needs and team coverage.
notes: Tests whether the system says no cap rather than a specific number. A wrong answer might say 20 days or unlimited — missing both the 15-day minimum expectation and the manager approval requirement.

question: How long is parental leave for the primary caregiver at Orion?
reference_answer: Primary caregivers receive 20 weeks of parental leave at 100% of base salary. This applies to all full-time employees regardless of gender or how they became a parent.
expected_chunk: Primary Caregiver Leave: 20 weeks, 100% of base salary, All full-time employees. Orion provides generous parental leave regardless of gender, parental role, or method of becoming a parent.
notes: Tests retrieval of exact duration. A wrong answer might say 12 weeks (a common statutory standard in other companies) or omit the 100% pay component.

question: What is the home office setup allowance for new full-time employees?
reference_answer: Full-time employees receive a one-time home office setup stipend of $1,500 upon joining and an annual equipment refresh allowance of $500 per year. Receipts must be submitted through Concur within 60 days of purchase.
expected_chunk: Full-time employees receive a one-time home office setup stipend of $1,500 upon joining and an annual equipment refresh allowance of $500 per year. Receipts must be submitted through Concur within 60 days of purchase.
notes: Tests whether both amounts are captured ($1,500 one-time and $500 annual). A wrong answer might give only one figure or omit the Concur 60-day submission requirement.

question: How much does Orion match in the 401(k) plan?
reference_answer: Orion matches 100% of employee contributions up to 5% of base salary. The employer match vests over 3 years: 33% after year one, 67% after year two, and 100% after year three.
expected_chunk: Orion matches 100% of employee contributions up to 5% of base salary. Employer match vests over 3 years: 33% after year 1, 67% after year 2, 100% after year 3.
notes: Two-part answer: match percentage AND vesting schedule. A wrong answer might state the match percentage but omit the 3-year vesting — leading an employee to wrongly assume the match is theirs immediately.

question: What notice period is required when an IC2 Software Engineer resigns?
reference_answer: Employees at the IC1 to IC3 level are expected to give 2 weeks notice when resigning.
expected_chunk: IC1 - IC3: 2 weeks [notice period upon voluntary resignation].
notes: Tests table retrieval. The question names IC2 specifically — the system must map IC2 into the IC1-IC3 band and return 2 weeks. A wrong answer might return the wrong band or give a generic answer without checking the level.

question: How many sick days are full-time employees entitled to each year?
reference_answer: Full-time employees receive 10 days (80 hours) of paid sick leave per calendar year. Sick leave does not carry over from year to year and is not paid out upon termination.
expected_chunk: Full-time employees receive 10 days (80 hours) of paid sick leave per calendar year. Sick leave does not carry over from year to year and is not paid out upon termination.
notes: Tests whether the no-carryover detail is included — it is the most practically important part of the policy. A wrong answer gives only the number of days without the carryover caveat.

question: How long is the probationary period for new employees at Orion?
reference_answer: All new regular employees serve a 90-day introductory period beginning on their first day of work. During this time they have access to standard benefits but cannot participate in the annual promotion cycle or tuition reimbursement.
expected_chunk: All new regular employees serve a 90-day introductory period beginning on their first day of work. During this time, employees have access to all standard benefits but may not participate in certain programs such as the annual promotion cycle or tuition reimbursement.
notes: Tests whether the system retrieves both the duration (90 days) and the excluded programs. Omitting the exclusions gives an incomplete picture of what new employees can and cannot access.

question: What is the phone number for Orion's Ethics Hotline?
reference_answer: The Ethics Hotline number is 1-800-676-3844. Reports can also be made online at oriontech.ethicspoint.com.
expected_chunk: Ethics Hotline: 1-800-ORN-ETHI (1-800-676-3844). Online: oriontech.ethicspoint.com.
notes: Tests specific data point retrieval. A hallucinating system might fabricate a plausible-looking number. Verify the answer contains 1-800-676-3844 exactly.

question: What are Orion's core hours — when all employees must be available?
reference_answer: Core hours at Orion are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in the employee's local time zone. All employees are expected to be available for meetings and collaboration during this window.
expected_chunk: Core hours -- when all employees are expected to be available for meetings and collaboration -- are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in the employee's local time zone.
notes: Tests a specific operational detail. A wrong answer might say 9 AM to 5 PM based on standard assumptions, or omit the local time zone qualifier that matters for remote and international employees.

question: When is the annual performance bonus paid at Orion?
reference_answer: The annual performance bonus is paid once per year in March, based on performance in the prior calendar year.
expected_chunk: Orion's annual performance bonus is paid once per year, in March, based on performance in the prior calendar year.
notes: Tests a specific date. A system that answers annually without specifying March is incomplete. Employees need the month to plan their finances.

question: What is the RSU vesting schedule at Orion?
reference_answer: RSUs vest over 4 years with a 1-year cliff: 25% vests after 12 months, then 1/48th per month for the remaining 36 months.
expected_chunk: Standard vesting schedule is 4 years with a 1-year cliff: 25% vests after 12 months, then 1/48 per month for the remaining 36 months.
notes: Tests whether the system captures the cliff structure correctly. A wrong answer might say 25% per year (simplified) rather than cliff at 12 months then monthly. The distinction matters if an employee is considering leaving at month 11.

question: How many paid holidays does Orion observe each year?
reference_answer: Orion observes 12 company-wide paid holidays per year.
expected_chunk: Orion observes 12 company-wide paid holidays per year.
notes: Simple single-fact retrieval. Tests whether the exact number 12 is returned rather than a guess. A wrong answer might say 10 or 11 based on common US standards.

question: How many paid days off does Orion provide for the death of a parent?
reference_answer: Orion provides 5 paid days of bereavement leave for the death of an immediate family member, which includes a spouse, child, parent, sibling, or grandparent.
expected_chunk: Immediate family (spouse, child, parent, sibling, grandparent): 5 paid days.
notes: Tests whether the system correctly classifies parent as immediate family and returns 5 days. A wrong answer might give 3 days (the extended family rate) or fail to distinguish between immediate and extended family categories.

question: How many therapy sessions per year does Orion's Employee Assistance Programme cover?
reference_answer: Orion's EAP, provided through Lyra Health, covers up to 25 therapy or counselling sessions per year. The service is confidential and free of charge for all employees and their household members.
expected_chunk: Up to 25 therapy or counselling sessions per year. EAP services are confidential and free of charge. All employees and their household members have access.
notes: Tests a specific benefit number. A wrong answer might say 10 sessions (a common EAP standard) rather than the actual 25. The household members scope is also often missed.

question: How much is the annual professional development budget per employee at Orion?
reference_answer: Orion provides $2,000 per year per employee for professional development. Approved uses include online courses, conferences, certifications, and books. Requests over $200 must be pre-approved through the People Portal.
expected_chunk: Professional Development Stipend: $2,000 per year for courses, books, or conferences. Requests over $200 must be pre-approved through the People Portal.
notes: Tests dollar amount plus pre-approval threshold. A common failure is stating the amount correctly but omitting the $200+ pre-approval rule — leaving employees to submit large expenses without the required sign-off.

question: What is the maximum value of a gift an Orion employee can accept from a vendor?
reference_answer: Employees may accept gifts with a fair market value of $75 or less. Cash and cash equivalents such as gift cards and vouchers may never be accepted regardless of their value.
expected_chunk: The gift or entertainment has a fair market value of USD $75 or less. It is not cash or a cash equivalent (gift cards, vouchers, etc.).
notes: Two-part rule: the $75 threshold AND the blanket ban on cash equivalents. A system that answers only $75 limit without mentioning the gift card prohibition gives an incomplete and potentially compliance-risky answer.

question: Is there a minimum length of service before an employee is eligible for the annual performance bonus?
reference_answer: Yes. Employees must have a minimum of three months of employment in the performance year, be actively employed on the payment date, and have a rating of Meets Expectations or above.
expected_chunk: A minimum of three months of employment in the performance year. Active employment on the date of payment. A performance rating of Meets Expectations or above.
notes: Tests whether the system returns all three eligibility conditions together. A partial answer that states only the tenure requirement misses the active employment and rating conditions that are equally important.

question: What is the minimum password length required by Orion's IT security policy?
reference_answer: All accounts at Orion must use strong, unique passwords with a minimum of 16 characters. Multi-Factor Authentication is also mandatory for all Orion systems.
expected_chunk: All accounts must use strong, unique passwords (minimum 16 characters). Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is mandatory for all Orion systems, including email, GitHub, AWS, and the VPN.
notes: Tests specific security policy retrieval. A wrong answer might say 8 or 12 characters based on common defaults. The 16-character requirement is above typical standards and matters for compliance verification.

question: How long does Orion's short-term disability coverage last and what percentage of salary does it pay?
reference_answer: Short-Term Disability pays 60% of base salary, up to $2,500 per week, for up to 12 weeks.
expected_chunk: Short-Term Disability (STD): 60% of base salary, up to $2,500/week, for up to 12 weeks.
notes: Three facts in one answer: percentage of pay (60%), weekly cap ($2,500), and maximum duration (12 weeks). A wrong answer typically gets the percentage right but omits the weekly cap or the duration.

question: How much notice does a Director need to give when resigning from Orion?
reference_answer: Directors and above are expected to give 6 weeks notice when resigning.
expected_chunk: Director and above: 6 weeks [notice period upon voluntary resignation].

question: I'm about to have a baby — how much paid leave will I get from Orion?
reference_answer: If you are the primary caregiver you receive 20 weeks at 100% base salary. If you are the secondary caregiver you receive 8 weeks at 100% base salary. Both apply to all full-time employees regardless of gender.
expected_chunk: Primary Caregiver Leave: 20 weeks, 100% of base salary. Secondary Caregiver Leave: 8 weeks, 100% of base salary. All full-time employees.
notes: Same answer as the primary caregiver factual row but conversational phrasing. Tests whether the embedding model connects about to have a baby with parental leave. A system too dependent on keyword matching may not retrieve the parental leave section.

question: If I'm not feeling well, how much paid time off can I take?

question: How do I report a concern about fraud at Orion without anyone knowing it was me?

question: Can Orion cover the cost of attending an industry conference?

question: Can an employee who just started and is still in their 90-day introductory period take parental leave?

question: If a Senior Engineer (IC3) gives exactly 2 weeks notice when resigning, will they be eligible for rehire at Orion in the future?
reference_answer: Yes. IC1-IC3 employees are expected to give 2 weeks notice. Orion only designates employees as ineligible for rehire if they resigned with less than two weeks notice — giving the full two weeks preserves rehire eligibility.

question: An employee joined Orion in October 2024 and received a Meets Expectations rating in the annual review. Will they receive a bonus in March 2025?

question: Does a part-time employee working 30 hours per week qualify for health insurance at Orion?
reference_answer: Yes. Part-Time Regular employees working 20 to 39 hours per week are eligible for pro-rated benefits. Full benefits eligibility begins on the first day of the month following the hire date.

Part-time employees working 20+ hours per week are eligible for pro-rated benefits.

question: An employee received a Developing rating (rating 2) in their annual review. Are they eligible for the performance bonus?

question: A conference costs $4,000 in total for registration and travel. How much can Orion cover?

question: A Director gives 6 weeks notice when resigning. Are they eligible for rehire at Orion in the future?

question: I want to work from Japan for 5 weeks while visiting family. What do I need to do before I go?
reference_answer: Working from a country other than your country of hire for more than 30 consecutive days requires prior written approval from the People team and Legal. Since 5 weeks is 35 days and exceeds the 30-day threshold, you must obtain written approval before going. Tax, immigration, and employment law implications vary by country and must be assessed in advance.
expected_chunk: Working from a country other than your country of hire for more than 30 consecutive days requires prior written approval from the People team and Legal. Tax, immigration, and employment law implications vary significantly by country and must be assessed in advance.
notes: Requires applying the 30-day policy rule to the arithmetic of 5 weeks = 35 days. A system that retrieves the policy but does not apply it to the specific scenario gives an incomplete answer. Tests reasoning over a retrieved fact, not just retrieval.

question: An employee received a verbal warning last month. Are they still eligible for the annual performance bonus?
reference_answer: Yes. A verbal warning alone does not affect bonus eligibility. The bonus requires active employment, at least 3 months in the performance year, and a Meets Expectations rating or above. A verbal warning is Step 1 of progressive discipline and does not change employment status or performance rating by itself.
expected_chunk: CHUNK 1 (p.10): Eligibility requires: Active employment on the date of payment. Minimum three months in the performance year. A performance rating of Meets Expectations or above. | CHUNK 2 (p.22): Step 1 - Verbal Warning: Manager discusses the issue, expectations, and required improvement. Documented in the employee's file.
notes: Requires connecting the disciplinary process (Section 12.1) with bonus eligibility criteria (Section 5.4). A verbal warning does not disqualify — only a rating below Meets Expectations would. A wrong answer might say the verbal warning makes them ineligible.

question: If an employee leaves Orion after 18 months of service, how much of the employer 401(k) match do they keep?

question: Is there any flexibility in the notice period if I want to leave sooner than expected?
reference_answer: Notice periods are set by level: 2 weeks for IC1-IC3, 4 weeks for IC4-IC5 and Managers, and 6 weeks for Directors and above. Orion may at its discretion choose to accept shorter notice — but this is Orion's decision, not the employee's right. Employees who resign with less than two weeks notice are designated as ineligible for rehire.
expected_chunk: Orion may, at its discretion, accept shorter notice, place the employee on garden leave for the notice period, or waive the notice period entirely. Employees who were terminated for cause or who resigned with less than two weeks notice are designated as ineligible for rehire.
notes: The word flexibility may cause the system to answer yes without the critical nuance: it is Orion's discretion to waive notice, not the employee's right to choose shorter notice. A correct answer must state this distinction clearly and include the rehire ineligibility consequence.

question: Can I use my home office stipend to buy the laptop I need to work from home?
reference_answer: No. Orion provides all full-time employees with a company-owned laptop separately from the home office stipend. The $1,500 stipend is for office setup items such as furniture and peripherals. Laptops remain company property and must be returned upon termination.
expected_chunk: Full-time employees receive a one-time home office setup stipend of $1,500 upon joining. Orion provides all employees with a company-owned laptop (MacBook Pro or equivalent). Laptops and other Orion-owned equipment remain company property and must be returned upon termination.
notes: The question assumes the stipend covers the laptop — it does not. A wrong answer might say yes, use the $1,500 for a laptop when the laptop is separately provided. This matters practically: buying a personal laptop with the stipend leaves the employee without the Orion-managed device they actually need.

question: Since Orion has a flexible PTO policy with no cap, can I take as many days as I want without asking my manager?
reference_answer: No. While the PTO policy has no preset cap, PTO must still be scheduled with manager approval taking into account business needs and team coverage. Flexible means no fixed limit — not that the approval process is optional.
expected_chunk: Orion provides a flexible PTO policy for all full-time exempt employees, meaning there is no preset cap on PTO days. PTO must be scheduled with manager approval, taking into account business needs and team coverage.
notes: Classic flexible PTO misinterpretation. The question plants the assumption that no cap means no process. The correct answer distinguishes between having no fixed limit and still requiring manager approval. A wrong answer confirms the assumption: yes take as many as you want.

question: My colleague needs urgent access to a system I manage while I am out sick. Can I share my password with them just this once?
reference_answer: No. Orion's security policy explicitly prohibits sharing passwords under any circumstances. Passwords must never be shared, written down, or stored in plain text. Urgent access requests should go through IT Security through the proper access provisioning process.
expected_chunk: Passwords must never be shared, written down, or stored in plain text. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is mandatory for all Orion systems.
notes: The urgency framing (just this once, out sick) is designed to make the exception seem reasonable. The handbook is unambiguous: never share passwords. A correct answer must say no clearly without hedging. A wrong answer might say generally not recommended but in emergencies consider it.

question: A vendor wants to thank me with an $80 gift card after a successful project. Can I accept it?
reference_answer: No. Orion's gifts policy has two separate rules that both apply here: the $80 value exceeds the $75 limit, AND gift cards are explicitly prohibited as cash equivalents regardless of their value. Both conditions independently prevent accepting this gift.
expected_chunk: The gift or entertainment has a fair market value of USD $75 or less. It is not cash or a cash equivalent (gift cards, vouchers, etc.). Gifts exceeding $75 in value must be declined.
notes: Double trap: $80 exceeds the $75 threshold AND gift cards are prohibited as cash equivalents at any value. A partial answer that flags only the $80 issue misses the gift card prohibition — which would also reject a $50 gift card. Both rules must be stated for the answer to be complete.

question: I have been genuinely unwell and missed 6 unplanned days in the past 90 days. Will I face disciplinary action?
reference_answer: Potentially yes. The handbook states that more than 5 unscheduled absences within any rolling 90-day period — excluding approved leave — will be addressed through the performance management process. Six days exceeds the threshold. However, if the absences were properly logged as approved sick leave, the excluding approved leave clause may apply.
expected_chunk: Excessive unplanned absences (more than 5 unscheduled days within any rolling 90-day period, excluding approved leave) will be addressed through the performance management process described in Section 12.
notes: The genuinely unwell framing might soften the system's response. The correct answer accurately states the 5-day threshold is exceeded while noting the excluding approved leave qualifier — which may protect the employee if absences were properly logged as sick leave. A wrong answer might only reassure the employee without flagging the threshold.
